# 🇱🇰 This Week in Sri Lanka

![LastUpdated](https://img.shields.io/badge/last_updated-2026--05--15_06:47:48-green)
[![RSS](https://img.shields.io/badge/RSS-Feed-orange)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nuuuwan/lk_news_digest/refs/heads/main/rss.xml)
[![Broadsheet](https://img.shields.io/badge/Broadsheet-View-darkred)](https://nuuuwan.github.io/lk_news_digest)

*Generated by gpt-5 from 154 English News Articles published between 2026-05-08 and 2026-05-15.*

## 1. Sri Lanka treated the $2.5 million Treasury transfer theft as a criminal breach while widening probes into external payment channels.

Investigators <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">examined email systems, authentication trails and devices</span>, and the Colombo Crime Division took over the case. The central banker said the institution executed client instructions and did not determine beneficiaries.

A parliamentary committee heard that the fraud <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">occurred across 10 remittances</span>, and that a separate payment stream linked to another country showed irregularities. Analysts said creditors viewed the episode as non‑sovereign, <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">avoiding a default label</span> but demanding tighter controls.

## 2. The Supreme Court curtailed a proposed tax recovery shortcut and preserved evidentiary rights in the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Bill 2026.

A bench ruled that a new <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">certificate‑to‑fine route</span> at Magistrates’ Courts would be unconstitutional unless recovery paused during administrative reviews and Appeals Commission hearings. It also recorded the Government’s withdrawal of a clause that would have <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">permanently excluded late‑produced documents</span> in disputes.

The Court <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">upheld criminalisation of persistent non‑filing</span> after a warning, with fines up to Rs. 400,000, as compatible with due process. Petitioners then withdrew three challenges, narrowing the remaining issues to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">recovery timing</span> and conclusive‑certificate language.

## 3. The central bank urged sustained reforms and stronger buffers, warning that shocks were becoming a permanent feature of the landscape.

Its economic review said growth at 5% in 2025 followed stabilisation, but the current account could widen in 2026 as imports rose faster than exports. It highlighted the need to keep a <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">market‑determined exchange rate</span> and build reserves to underpin external stability.

Directors cited risks from a prolonged West Asian war to activity and revenue, even as remittances and services supported the external sector. The review targeted <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">revenue above 15% of GDP</span> and a budget deficit below 5% by 2027, following <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">external debt restructuring</span> that lowered interest outflows.

## 4. The Cabinet moved to tighten environmental regulation with pollutant charges, mandatory location screening and legally binding management plans.

Draft amendments introduced <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">load‑based charging</span> so fees reflected volumes discharged rather than flat rates. Developers would have to obtain <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">site‑specific environmental recommendations</span> before operations and file enforceable Environmental Management Plans.

The bill also formalised <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Strategic Environmental Assessments (SEA, early‑stage planning appraisals)</span> and created licensing for <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">hazardous chemicals</span>, with non‑compliance exposing firms to fines or imprisonment of up to two years. Regulators said the framework targeted wastewater‑intensive and hazardous sectors in manufacturing, energy, construction and chemicals.

## 5. Parliament’s accounts committee flagged a Rs. 167 million loss tied to a 2013 loan and unrecovered cooperative debts up to 59 years old.

Officials told members that repayments on the building materials loan had stopped in 2015 after the borrower began incurring losses. Auditors also identified <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Rs. 31 million outstanding</span> from 28 societies, with recovery hampered where entities were registered under provincial statutes.

## 6. Ministers approved an emergency order for 865,000 vials of meropenem worth $908,250 as hospital demand rose.

The supplementary procurement <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">kept the original per‑vial price</span> to avoid delays and cost escalation. The antibiotic, widely used in intensive care for <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">drug‑resistant infections</span>, followed an October 2025 approval for 1.9 million vials.

## 7. The United States cleared Sri Lankan blue swimming crab shipments after granting a Marine Mammal Protection Act comparability finding.

The <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">NOAA certification</span> under the MMPA (a US law requiring foreign fisheries to meet marine mammal protection standards) runs <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">to 31 December 2029</span>. World Bank data showed over $16 million in prepared crab exports in 2023, with more than $12 million sent to the US.

## 8. A joint storage venture secured investment for 12 grid‑scale battery projects after a revised capital structure.

An energy developer agreed to buy 49% of Storex for <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">about Rs. 2 billion</span>, while its partner retained 51%. The portfolio comprised <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">10MW/40MWh</span> Battery Energy Storage System units (BESS, grid‑connected batteries that balance supply and demand) at a dozen locations awarded by the power utility.

## 9. An appellate tribunal ordered a retrospective promotion for a former police investigations head to the rank of DIG from August 2020.

The <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">Administrative Appeals Tribunal</span> directed the police commission to <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">adjust seniority</span> and service records accordingly. The officer had joined the service in 1986 and served across multiple ranks before the disputed promotion window.

## 10. The President instructed officials to accelerate land identification and relocation for Cyclone Ditwah‑displaced families in the hills.

District data recorded <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">61,833 affected people</span> from 18,266 families and <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">93 deaths</span>, with more than 2,900 houses fully or partially destroyed. Agencies were told to prioritise <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">estate workers still in tents</span> by building <span style="background-color:#EBEBEB;border-radius:3px;padding:0 2px">temporary shelters</span> on the designated plots until permanent housing was completed.
